post.description:
Delete Instance

 Deletes an instance with the given ID.
 This method requires system level permissions and cannot be called from an instance context.

 Required permissions:
   - `system.instance.delete`

Get Instance

 Returns the instance in the current context or by its ID.
 By default the instance will be determined by the context of the request,
 e.g. the host header.
 You can optionally pass an InstanceID to retrieve a specific instance.
 This requires additional permissions.

 Required permissions:
   - `iam.read`
   - `system.instance.read` (if InstanceID is set)

Update Instance

 Updates instance's name in the current context or by its ID.
 By default the instance will be determined by the context of the request,
 e.g. the host header.
 You can optionally pass an InstanceID to update a specific instance.
 This requires additional permissions.

 Required permissions:
   - `iam.write`
   - `system.instance.write` (if InstanceID is set)

List Instances

 Lists instances matching the given query.
 The query can be used to filter either by instance ID or domain.
 The request is paginated and returns 100 results by default.
 This method requires system level permissions and cannot be called from an instance context.

 Required permissions:
  - `system.instance.read`

Add Custom Domain

 Adds a Custom Domain to the instance.
 The Custom Domain must be unique across all instances.
 Once the domain is added, it will be used to route requests to this instance.
 This method requires system level permissions and cannot be called from an instance context.

 Required permissions:
   - `system.domain.write`

Remove Custom Domain

 Removes a Custom Domain from the instance.
 Be aware that this will stop routing requests from this domain to the instance and
 might break existing setups or integrations.
 This method requires system level permissions and cannot be called from an instance context.

 Required permissions:
  - `system.domain.write`

List Custom Domains

 Lists Custom Domains of the instance.

 By default the instance will be determined by the context of the request,
 e.g. the host header.
 You can optionally pass an InstanceID to list the domains of a specific instance.
 This requires additional permissions.

 Required permissions:
  - `iam.read`
  - `system.instance.read` (if InstanceID is set)

Add Trusted Domain

 Adds a Trusted Domain to the instance.
 By default the instance will be determined by the context of the request,
 e.g. the host header.
 You can optionally pass an InstanceID to list the domains of a specific instance.
 This requires additional permissions.

 It must be a valid domain name.
 Once the domain is added, it can be used in API responses like OIDC discovery,
 email templates, and more.
 This can be used in cases where the API is accessed through a different domain
 than the Custom Domain, e.g. proxy setups and custom login UIs.
 Unlike Custom Domains, Trusted Domains are not used to route requests to this instance
 and therefore do not need to be uniquely assigned to an instance.

 Required permissions:
  - `iam.write`
  - `system.instance.write` (if InstanceID is set)

Remove Trusted Domain

 Removes a Trusted Domain from the instance.
 By default the instance will be determined by the context of the request,
 e.g. the host header.
 You can optionally pass an InstanceID to list the domains of a specific instance.
 This requires additional permissions.

 Required permissions:
  - `iam.write`
  - `system.instance.write` (if InstanceID is set)

List Trusted Domains

 Lists Trusted Domains of the instance.
 By default the instance will be determined by the context of the request,
 e.g. the host header.
 You can optionally pass an InstanceID to list the domains of a specific instance.
 This requires additional permissions.

 Required permissions:
   - `iam.read`
   - `system.instance.read` (if InstanceID is set)

ids.title: ids
ids.description: Defines the ids to query for.

offset.title: offset
offset.description: Starting point for retrieval, in combination of offset used to query a set list of objects.

limit.title: limit
limit.description:
limit is the maximum amount of objects returned. The default is set to 100
 with a maximum of 1000 in the runtime configuration.
 If the limit exceeds the maximum configured Zitadel will throw an error.
 If no limit is present the default is taken.

asc.title: asc
asc.description:
Asc is the sorting order. If true the list is sorted ascending, if false
 the list is sorted descending. The default is descending.

totalResult.title: total_result
totalResult.description: Absolute number of objects matching the query, regardless of applied limit.

appliedLimit.title: applied_limit
appliedLimit.description: Applied limit from query, defines maximum amount of objects per request, to compare if all objects are returned.

instanceId.title: instance_id
instanceId.description: InstanceID is the unique ID of the instance to which the domain will be added.

customDomain.title: custom_domain
customDomain.description:
Custom Domain to add to the instance.
 Must be a valid domain name.
 Once the domain is added, it will be used to route requests to this instance.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: CreationDate is the timestamp when the domain was added.

instanceId.title: instance_id
instanceId.description:
InstanceID is the unique ID of the instance to which the Trusted Domain will be added.
 If not set, the instance in the current context (e.g. identified by the host header) will be used.
 If an ID is set, the caller must have additional permissions.

trustedDomain.title: trusted_domain
trustedDomain.description:
Trusted Domain to be added to the instance.
 Must be a valid domain name.
 Once the domain is added, it can be used in API responses like OIDC discovery,
 email templates, and more.
 This can be used in cases where the API is accessed through a different domain
 than the Custom Domain, e.g. proxy setups and custom login UIs.
 Unlike Custom Domains, Trusted Domains are not used to route requests to this instance
 and therefore do not need to be uniquely assigned to an instance.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: CreationDate is the timestamp when the Trusted Domain was added.

instanceId.title: instance_id
instanceId.description: InstanceID is the unique identifier of the instance the domain belongs to.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: CreationDate is the timestamp when the domain was created.

domain.title: domain
domain.description:
Domain is the fully qualified domain name.
 It must be unique across all instances in the system.

primary.title: primary
primary.description:
Primary states whether this domain is the primary domain of the instance.
 Each instance must have exactly one primary domain.
 The primary domain is used for various purposes and acts as fallback
 in those cases, e.g if no explicit domain is specified.

generated.title: generated
generated.description:
Generate states whether this domain was auto-generated by the system.
 Auto-generated domains follow a specific pattern and are created
 when a new instance is created.
 They cannot be deleted, but the primary domain can be changed
 to a manually added domain.

domainFilter.title: domain_filter
domainFilter.description: Filter for a specific Custom Domain.

generatedFilter.title: generated_filter
generatedFilter.description: Filter whether the domain is auto-generated.

primaryFilter.title: primary_filter
primaryFilter.description: Filter whether the domain is the primary domain of the instance.

domains.title: domains
domains.description:
The domains to query for. All instances that have at least one of the
 specified domains will be returned.
 A maximum of 20 domains can be specified.

instanceId.title: instance_id
instanceId.description: InstanceID is the unique ID of the instance to be deleted.

deletionDate.title: deletion_date
deletionDate.description: DeletionDate is the timestamp when the instance was deleted.

method.title: method
method.description:
The method to use for text comparison.
 If not specified, EQUALS is used.

customDomainsFilter.title: custom_domains_filter
customDomainsFilter.description: Filter for instances that have at least one of the specified Custom Domains.

inIdsFilter.title: in_ids_filter
inIdsFilter.description: Filter for one or more specific instance IDs.

instanceId.title: instance_id
instanceId.description:
InstanceID is the unique ID of the instance to be retrieved.
 If not set, the instance in the current context (e.g. identified by the host header) will be returned.
 If an ID is set, the caller must have additional permissions.

instance.title: instance
instance.description: The instance matching the instance ID

id.title: id
id.description: ID is the unique identifier of the instance.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: ChangeDate is the timestamp when the instance was last changed.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: CreationDate is the timestamp when the instance was created.

state.title: state
state.description: State is the current state of the instance.

name.title: name
name.description:
Name is the display name of the instance.
 This can be changed by the instance administrator.

version.title: version
version.description:
Version of the system the instance is running on.
 This is managed by the system and cannot be changed by the instance administrator.

customDomains.title: custom_domains
customDomains.description:
CustomDomains are the domains that are assigned to the instance.
 The list includes auto-generated and manually added domains.
 They are all unique across all instances in the system.
 They're used to route requests to the correct instance.

instanceId.title: instance_id
instanceId.description:
InstanceID is the unique ID of the instance  whose domains will be listed.
 If not set, the instance in the current context (e.g. identified by the host header) will be used.
 If an ID is set, the caller must have additional permissions.

pagination.title: pagination
pagination.description: Paginate and sort the results using a limit, offset and sorting.

sortingColumn.title: sorting_column
sortingColumn.description: The field the result is sorted by.

filters.title: filters
filters.description: Filter the domains to be returned.

domains.title: domains
domains.description: The list of Custom Domains matching the query.

pagination.title: pagination
pagination.description: Contains the total number of domains matching the query and the applied limit.

pagination.title: pagination
pagination.description: Paginate through the results using a limit, offset and sorting.

sortingColumn.title: sorting_column
sortingColumn.description: The field the result is sorted by.

filters.title: filters
filters.description: Filter the instances to be returned.

pagination.title: pagination
pagination.description: Contains the total number of instances matching the query and the applied limit.

instanceId.title: instance_id
instanceId.description:
InstanceID is the unique ID of the instance whose Trusted Domains will be listed.
 If not set, the instance in the current context (e.g. identified by the host header) will be used.
 If an ID is set, the caller must have additional permissions.

pagination.title: pagination
pagination.description: Paginate and sort the results using a limit, offset and sorting.

sortingColumn.title: sorting_column
sortingColumn.description: The field the result is sorted by.

filters.title: filters
filters.description: Filter the domains to be returned.

trustedDomain.title: trusted_domain
trustedDomain.description: The list of Trusted Domains matching the query.

pagination.title: pagination
pagination.description: Contains the total number of domains matching the query and the applied limit.

instanceId.title: instance_id
instanceId.description: InstanceID is the unique ID of the instance from which the domain will be removed.

customDomain.title: custom_domain
customDomain.description: CustomDomain is the the domain to remove from the instance.

deletionDate.title: deletion_date
deletionDate.description: DeletionDate is the timestamp when the domain was removed.

instanceId.title: instance_id
instanceId.description:
InstanceID is the unique ID of the instance from which the Trusted Domain will be removed.
 If not set, the instance in the current context (e.g. identified by the host header) will be used.
 If an ID is set, the caller must have additional permissions.

trustedDomain.title: trusted_domain
trustedDomain.description: The Trusted Domain to remove from the instance.

deletionDate.title: deletion_date
deletionDate.description: DeletionDate is the timestamp when the Trusted Domain was removed.

instanceId.title: instance_id
instanceId.description: InstanceID is the unique identifier of the instance the domain belongs to.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: The timestamp when the domain was created.

domain.title: domain
domain.description: Domain is the fully qualified domain name.

domainFilter.title: domain_filter
domainFilter.description: Filter for a specific Trusted Domain.

instanceId.title: instance_id
instanceId.description:
InstanceID is the unique ID of the instance to be updated.
 If not set, the instance in the current context (e.g. identified by the host header) will be changed.
 If an ID is set, the caller must have additional permissions.

instanceName.title: instance_name
instanceName.description: InstanceName is the new name of the instance to be set.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description:
ChangeDate is the timestamp when the instance was last changed.
 In case the instance was not changed during the call, the previous change date is returned.

connect-protocol-version.title: Connect-Protocol-Version
connect-protocol-version.description: Define the version of the Connect protocol

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of [google.rpc.Code][google.rpc.Code].

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the [google.rpc.Status.details][google.rpc.Status.details] field, or localized by the client.

google.protobuf.Any.description: Contains an arbitrary serialized message along with a @type that describes the type of the serialized message.

tags.name: zitadel.instance.v2.InstanceService
tags.description:
Service to manage instances and their domains.
 The service provides methods to create, update, delete and list instances and their domains.

 Contrary to most services in ZITADEL, the instance service allows accessing data not only from
 the current instance, but also from other instances.
